description: Implement Find & Replace across all translations with a modal dialog in Phase 4, integration with the undo stack from FEAT-001, and a CLI 'stx replace' command for batch operations.
steps: Create src/stx/gui/find_replace_dialog.py with a FindReplaceDialog(QDialog) class that has: (1) Find QLineEdit, (2) Replace QLineEdit, (3) Match Case checkbox, (4) Regex checkbox, (5) Scope combo (Translations Only / Labels / Keys / All Fields), (6) a preview count label showing how many matches exist, (7) Replace All button, (8) Cancel button. The dialog should accept the Document and return a list of (row_index, old_value, new_value) tuples when Replace All is clicked.
Add a 'Global Replace...' QPushButton to the Phase4ReviewPage toolbar (after the 'Load reviewed Excel...' button). Clicking opens the FindReplaceDialog modally.

In Phase4ReviewPage, implement _on_find_replace() that: (1) opens the dialog, (2) if accepted, iterates the returned replacements, (3) for each replacement pushes an UndoCommand onto the undo stack and applies the change via the model, (4) calls _update_counters() and _run_auto_validation() after all replacements are done, (5) shows a status message like 'Replaced N occurrences'.
Add a menu entry 'Find && Replace...' (Ctrl+H) to the Edit menu in main_window.py that triggers the same dialog when Phase 4 is active.

In the inline editor at the bottom of Phase 4, add an 'Apply to all rows' checkbox. When checked and the user clicks 'Apply', the current edit is treated as a find-replace: the OLD translation of the selected row is searched across all rows, and any matching translations are replaced with the new text. This uses the same undo-stack-aware replacement logic.

Add a CLI command 'stx replace' in src/stx/cli.py that accepts: source file (.stf or .xlsx), --find TEXT, --replace TEXT, --case-sensitive flag, --regex flag, --scope (translation/label/key/all). It loads the document, performs the replacement, saves back, and prints how many replacements were made.

Add tests in tests/gui/test_find_replace.py: (1) test FindReplaceDialog construction, (2) test replacement logic with plain text, (3) test replacement with regex, (4) test case-sensitive vs case-insensitive matching, (5) test scope filtering (translations only vs all fields), (6) test that replacements integrate with undo stack (can be undone as a batch).
Add CLI tests in tests/test_cli.py: test_replace_plain, test_replace_regex, test_replace_case_sensitive.
acceptance_criteria: FindReplaceDialog exists and can be opened from the Phase 4 toolbar
Find & Replace correctly replaces text across all matching translations
Match Case and Regex options work correctly
Scope control limits replacements to the chosen field(s)
Preview count updates as the user types in Find field
All replacements from a single Find & Replace operation can be undone with a single Ctrl+Z (or multiple Ctrl+Z one per row)
Apply to all rows checkbox in inline editor performs global replacement
CLI 'stx replace' command works for batch operations on .stf and .xlsx files
Edit menu has Find & Replace (Ctrl+H) entry
findings: Shared utility in src/stx/find_replace.py decouples replacement logic from both GUI and CLI. The dialog uses a debounce timer (150ms) for preview updates. All 198 tests pass (30 new). The 'Apply to all rows' checkbox uses case-sensitive exact matching by default since it replaces the exact old translation value. Replacement through GUI pushes individual UndoCommands per row so each can be individually undone with Ctrl+Z.
